steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between the sentences
steps.content: The first sentence establishes a positive quality of the material — its flexibility aids movement. The second sentence introduces an opposing negative quality — the weight of the embroidered feathers limits mobility. These two ideas stand in direct contrast, so the transition word must signal opposition.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Evaluate each answer choice against that requirement:
  • Choice A — 'However,': signals contrast or complication of the previous point, fitting the opposition between the material’s flexibility benefit and the weight limitation. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Firstly,': signals the first item in an enumerated list, but no list is being introduced here. ✗
  • Choice C — 'In other words,': signals restatement or clarification, but the second sentence introduces a new opposing idea rather than restating the first. ✗
  • Choice D — 'For this reason,': signals that the second sentence is a consequence of the first, but limited mobility is not caused by flexibility — they are opposing qualities. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
